items.q: What is Peptid Tracker?
items.a: Peptid Tracker is a personal app for research use. You can manage your peptides, plan intake cycles, log doses, calculate dosing, record effects in the journal, and write reviews—all in one place.

items.q: How do I move between sections?
items.a: At the bottom of the screen there is navigation with 5 icons: Stock, Peptides, Home (center), Calendar, and Profile. You reach all other areas from the Home screen in the middle.

items.q: What is the Home screen?
a: The Home screen (middle button in the navigation) is your hub:
• At the top you see 3 quick stats: Active cycles, Vials in stock, My peptides
• Below that are tiles for all 8 areas of the app
• Tap a tile to jump there directly

items.q: What are all the areas of the app?
a: 📅 Calendar – daily log, confirm doses & cycle overview
📦 Stock – raw material inventory, store & manage vials
🧪 Peptides – reconstituted peptides & create cycles
🧮 Calculator – dose calculator with syringe scale
📓 Journal – record effects and side effects
⭐ Reviews – experience reports for individual peptides
👤 Profile – account data, public profile & share link
❓ FAQ – this help page

items.q: How do I sign out?
items.a: Go to “Profile” and tap the red “Sign out” button at the top right.

items.q: Is my data stored securely?
items.a: Yes. All data is stored in a Supabase database. Each user only sees their own data—this is enforced with Row Level Security (RLS). Batch files (PDFs/images) live in a separate storage bucket and are only accessible to you.

items.q: Can I install the app on my phone?
a: Yes! Peptid Tracker is a PWA (Progressive Web App):
iPhone/Safari: Share icon → “Add to Home Screen” → “Add”
Android/Chrome: Three dots → “Install app” or “Add to Home screen”
The app then runs without the browser chrome and feels like a native app.

items.q: Where should I start?
a: Suggested order:
1. “Peptides” → “+ New” → create a peptide (name, active ingredient, reconstitution, stock)
2. “Add cycle” directly on the peptide card
3. Use “Calculator” to compute units & concentration
4. Open “Calendar” – the cycle appears with a purple background
5. Tap a cycle day → log dose & confirm

items.q: What does the calendar show?
a: The calendar gives you an at-a-glance overview:
🟣 Purple background = active cycle planned for that day
🔵 Blue dot = a dose was logged for that day
🔵 Sky ring = today
🟠 Orange arrow icon = a dose increase is active on that day

items.q: How do I log a dose?
a: 1. Tap a day in the calendar
2. Active cycles appear as cards in the day panel below
3. Tap a cycle → the log form opens prefilled
4. Adjust dose, method, or time if needed
5. Tap “Save”

items.q: What is dose confirmation?
a: After logging you can confirm each dose:
✅ “Taken” – entry is marked green
❌ “Not taken” – entry is marked red and snooze options appear
Until you confirm, both buttons appear on the entry card.

items.q: What is snooze?
a: When you tap “Not taken”, snooze buttons appear:
⏰ 15 min – reminder in 15 minutes
⏰ 30 min – reminder in 30 minutes
⏰ 1 h – reminder in 1 hour
⏰ 2 h – reminder in 2 hours
When it fires, a toast shows the peptide and dose.

items.q: What does the orange arrow in the calendar mean?
items.a: The orange arrow (📈 increase active) means a dose increase from your cycle applies that day. The dose shown in the day panel is already the increased total dose.

items.q: How do I move between months?
items.a: Tap the arrows left/right of the month name.

items.q: Can I delete a logged dose?
items.a: Yes. In the day panel there is an ✕ button to the right of each entry → tap it and confirm.

items.q: Why is there no purple background even though I have a cycle?
a: Possible reasons:
• Cycle is “Inactive” → in Peptides → cycle → turn the switch on
• Wrong month shown → browse to the cycle’s start month
• Start/end dates exclude this month

items.q: How do I create a new peptide?
a: 1. Tap “+ New” at the top right
2. Enter a name or pick from “Known”
3. Fill in active ingredient & reconstitution (mg/vial, liquid, syringe)
4. Enter stock, batch info, and dosing
5. Optionally upload a PDF or image of the analysis document
6. Tap “Save”

items.q: What does the animated vial on the peptide card show?
a: If you entered stock, an animated vial appears on the left of the card:
🟢 Green = more than 50% stock left
🟡 Yellow = 25–50% stock
🔴 Red = less than 25% – running low soon
The liquid animates. On phones the vial tilts with device orientation.

items.q: What is the info button (note icon) on the peptide card?
a: The note icon (📄) opens an info sheet with all saved data:
• Dose & route
• Active ingredient, liquid volume, syringe
• Reconstitution date & expiry with countdown
• Stock & progress bar
• Batch number & source
• Analysis document: images inline, PDFs as a link
• Notes

items.q: What is stock management?
a: You can enter how many vials you have:
• “Vials on hand” = current stock
• On first save this value is remembered as the 100% baseline
• The progress bar on the card shows consumption in color
• Expiry is calculated from reconstitution date + shelf life

items.q: What is batch information?
a: Batch information documents where your peptide came from:
• Batch number = manufacturer lot ID
• Source = manufacturer or supplier (e.g. “Peptide Sciences”)
• Analysis document = upload PDF or image (COA, lab report, invoice)
This also appears in the peptide info sheet.

items.q: What does “Added liquid (mL)” mean?
items.a: That is how much water (e.g. BAC water, NaCl, or sterile water for injection) you add to the vial. More liquid means lower concentration. Typical values are 1–2 mL.

items.q: What do the syringe fields “mL” and “units” mean?
a: These two fields describe your syringe:
• mL = total syringe volume (e.g. 1 mL)
• Units = maximum scale marks (e.g. 100 on a U-100 syringe)
→ From that: units/mL = marks per milliliter
Standard U-100 insulin syringe: 1 mL / 100 units = 100 units/mL

items.q: What is shelf life after reconstitution?
a: After dissolving the peptide it is only stable for a limited time (in the fridge):
10–14 days = short-lived peptides
21–28 days = typical for reconstituted peptides
42–90 days = especially stable peptides
Expiry is calculated from reconstitution date + selected days and shown in color.

items.q: How do I add a cycle from the peptide card?
items.a: Each peptide card has a purple “Add cycle” button at the bottom right. Tap it—you don’t need to expand the peptide first.

items.q: What does the arrow with cycle count at the bottom show?
items.a: The small arrow at the bottom left (e.g. “▼ 2 cycles”) expands or collapses the cycle view. You see at a glance how many cycles exist for this peptide.

items.q: How do I search for a peptide?
items.a: Once peptides exist, a search field appears at the top. Type a name—the list filters automatically. Use the dropdown next to it to sort A→Z or Z→A.

items.q: What can the calculator do?
a: From your inputs the calculator computes:
• Units to draw – how many marks on the syringe
• Concentration – mg/mL of the finished solution
• Syringe fill – what percent of the syringe you draw
• Doses per vial – how many injections you get from one vial

items.q: What is the syringe scale?
a: The colored scale at the top shows visually how many units to draw:
• The bar fills left (blue) to right (purple → pink)
• The white line marks the exact point
• The large number above shows units
You immediately see whether your dose fits in the syringe.

items.q: What inputs does the calculator need?
a: • Syringe size – pick a preset (e.g. 1 mL / 100 units) or enter custom values
• Active per vial – mg on the vial (e.g. 10 mg)
• Added liquid – how many mL you added (e.g. 2 mL)
• Dose – your target dose with unit (mcg, mg, IU)

items.q: Which syringe presets exist?
a: • 1 mL · 100 units (U-100) – standard insulin syringe
• 0.5 mL · 50 units (U-100) – small insulin syringe
• 0.3 mL · 30 units (U-100) – very small syringe
• 2 mL · 200 units (U-100) – larger syringe
• 1 mL · 40 units (U-40) – older U-40 syringe
Or: enter custom mL and units.

items.q: Example – how does the math work?
a: Example: BPC-157, 5 mg vial, 2 mL water, 500 mcg dose, U-100 syringe
→ Concentration: 5 mg ÷ 2 mL = 2.5 mg/mL = 2500 mcg/mL
→ Volume: 500 mcg ÷ 2500 mcg/mL = 0.200 mL
→ Units: 0.200 mL × 100 units/mL = 20 units
→ Doses/vial: 5000 mcg ÷ 500 mcg = 10 doses

items.q: What is a cycle?
items.a: A cycle is a structured intake plan for a peptide. It defines dose, method, frequency, time range, optional intake time, and reminders.

items.q: How do I create a cycle?
a: 1. On the peptide card tap “+ Add cycle” (purple button)
2. Fill in name, dose, frequency, and dates
3. Optionally set intake time and reminders
4. Tap “Save”
The cycle appears in the calendar automatically!

items.q: Which frequency options exist?
a: • Daily · Twice daily · Every other day
• 5 days on / 2 off (5on/2off)
• Mon–Fri · Weekly
• Every X days – custom interval
• Pick weekdays – e.g. only Mon, Wed, Fri

items.q: What does the Active/Inactive switch mean?
items.a: Active = cycle shows in the calendar (purple days). Inactive = cycle paused, not visible in the calendar. Toggle by tapping the switch on the right of the cycle.

items.q: What is intake time?
a: Optional – sets time of day:
🌅 Morning = 08:00 · ☀️ Noon = 12:00 · 🌙 Evening = 20:00 · 🕐 Custom time
Used for reminders. It is optional—you can leave it empty.

items.q: How do reminders work?
a: Reminders are multi-select—you can pick several:
• 1 day before – reminder 24 hours before intake
• 2 h before – 2 hour lead time
• At intake – exactly at the set time
The app asks for notification permission when you save.
Important: only works while the app is open.

items.q: Can I have multiple cycles for one peptide?
items.a: Yes, as many as you like. All active cycles show in the calendar. Useful e.g. for morning + evening or different dosing phases.

items.q: What is a dose increase?
items.a: A planned rise in dose within a cycle. Example: start at 200 mcg, after 2 weeks +100 mcg, after 4 weeks another +100 mcg. Multiple steps are possible.

items.q: How do I add a dose increase?
a: 1. Expand peptide → find cycle → “Dose increases” section
2. Tap “+ Add”
3. Enter increase amount and unit
4. Pick start: fixed date / after X days / after X weeks
5. Optionally add a note → Save

items.q: Is the dose increase shown in the calendar?
a: Yes! From when an increase applies:
• Orange 📈 in the day panel shows “Stage X active”
• The displayed dose is already the increased total (base + increase)
• The increase icon appears in the calendar legend

items.q: What do the start options mean?
a: • Fixed date – from which day the increase applies
• After X days – X days after cycle start
• After X weeks – equivalent days after cycle start

items.q: Can I have multiple steps?
items.a: Yes, as many as you like. They are numbered #1, #2, #3. All active steps add together.

items.q: What is the journal?
items.a: Here you document effects and side effects of your peptides. It helps you spot patterns—which effects occur when, how strong, and how long they last.

items.q: What is the difference between effect and side effect?
a: ✅ Effect (green) = desired outcome (sleep, healing, energy...)
⚠️ Side effect (orange) = unwanted outcome (pain, fatigue...)

items.q: What do the status options mean?
a: 🔘 Pending – not yet occurred
✅ Occurred – actively present
⏳ Still ongoing – continues
✅ Faded – over
Change status on the card directly without opening the form.

items.q: What is the intensity scale (1–5)?
a: 1 = Barely noticeable · 2 = Mild · 3 = Moderate · 4 = Strong · 5 = Very strong

items.q: How do I filter and search the journal?
a: • Tabs: All / Effects / Side effects
• Search: filters by description and peptide name
• Sort: date (new/old), intensity (high/low)

items.q: What are reviews?
items.a: Personal experience reports for individual peptides. With stars (1–5), overall experience (good/medium/bad), pros and cons, and a detailed write-up.

items.q: How do I create a review?
a: 1. In “Reviews” tap “+ New”
2. Pick peptide → assign stars → pick experience
3. Enter title (required) → optional report, pros, cons
4. Save

items.q: How do I search and sort reviews?
a: • Search: title and peptide name
• Sort: newest / oldest / rating high / rating low

items.q: Can I share reviews on my profile?
items.a: Yes. In “Profile” turn on the “Reviews” switch—they then appear on your public profile link.

items.q: What can I enter in my profile?
a: • Username (for the share link) – required
• Display name, age, sex, weight, height
• Personal notes (only for you)
• Public bio (shown on the shared profile)

items.q: How do I enable the public profile?
a: 1. Enter username and save profile
2. Turn on the main “Share profile” switch
3. Enable individual areas (Peptides / Calendar / Journal / Reviews)
4. Save → link appears and can be copied

items.q: Which content can I share?
a: Each area has its own switch:
🧪 Peptides · 📅 Calendar & cycles · 📖 Journal · ⭐ Reviews
You can e.g. share only reviews and keep everything else private.

items.q: Can I turn off sharing anytime?
items.a: Yes. Turn main “Share profile” off → save. The link immediately shows “This profile is private”.

items.q: How do I set up reminders?
a: 1. Create or edit a cycle
2. Set intake time (morning/noon/evening/custom)
3. Under “Reminder” pick one or more options (multi-select)
4. Save → app asks for notification permission

items.q: Can I pick multiple reminder times?
items.a: Yes. You can e.g. enable “1 day before” and “At intake” at the same time. Checkmarks show which are active.

items.q: What is the difference between reminder and snooze?
a: Reminder (in cycle) = planned notification before intake
Snooze (in calendar) = follow-up reminder after you mark a dose “Not taken” (15 min / 30 min / 1 h / 2 h)

items.q: Why am I not getting reminders?
a: • Notification permission denied → enable in phone settings
• App was not open at reminder time
• Reminder time for today has already passed
• Cycle is “Inactive”

items.q: Do reminders work when the app is closed?
items.a: Not currently. Notifications are browser-based and need an open app (tab or PWA). Background delivery would need a push service.

items.q: Why do I see “Error saving”?
a: • No internet connection
• Required fields missing
• Session expired → sign out and back in
• PDF upload: storage bucket not set up yet → run SQL in Supabase

items.q: Why can’t I upload a PDF?
a: The “batch-files” storage bucket must be set up once in Supabase:
1. supabase.com → your project → SQL Editor → new tab
2. Paste and run the SQL from “supabase-inventory.sql”
Uploads work immediately afterward.

items.q: What happens to my data when I sign out?
items.a: Your data stays on the server. After the next login all entries are still there.

items.q: Is data deleted if I uninstall the app?
items.a: No. Data lives on the server (Supabase)—independent of the device. Just sign in again on any device.

items.q: Is the app suitable for medical use?
items.a: No. For research and documentation only. Not a substitute for medical advice. Always consult a doctor.

items.q: Can I use the app on a tablet or second device?
a: Yes. Because everything is in the cloud, the app works on any number of devices:
1. Open the same URL in the browser
2. Sign in with the same account
3. All data is available immediately
For code access (development): clone the repository on GitHub.
